nodes.en: Fundamental chemical processes and reactions applicable to survival—including soap-making, fermentation, water disinfection, and soil amendment. Operates at the scale of household and field rather than industrial synthesis; enables food preservation, hygiene, and agricultural productivity distinct from medicine which focuses on disease treatment.
nodes.cs: Základní chemické procesy a reakce použitelné pro přežití—včetně výroby mýdla, fermentace, dezinfekce vody a úpravy půdy. Pracuje v měřítku domácností a polí spíše než průmyslové syntézy; umožňuje konzervaci potravin, hygienu a zemědělskou produktivitu odlišně od medicíny zaměřené na léčbu chorob.
nodes.brief:
Essential Chemistry is the set of household- and field-scale chemical processes — soap-making, fermentation, water disinfection, and soil amendment — that a rebuilding group needs once industrial chemical supply chains are gone. It draws on the tools [[12.02]] Tool Making provides and the power and water [[12.01]] Basic Infrastructure supplies, operating at the scale of a household or field rather than an industrial plant; its outputs in turn feed [[12.04]] Basic Medicine Production, which needs antiseptics and solvents this subfield produces.

Elsewhere in the corpus, [[01.02]] Chemistry covers the same acid-base, fermentation, and oxidation reactions with full academic and industrial rigor and instrumentation. Essential Chemistry is that same science reduced to what can be practiced with household apparatus, empirically verified results, and locally available reagents rather than a laboratory supply catalog.

Lewis Dartnell's [*The Knowledge: How to Rebuild Our World from Scratch*](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Knowledge:_How_to_Rebuild_Our_World_From_Scratch) devotes chapters to exactly this scale of chemistry — soap, acids, and preservation from first principles — and the [Wikipedia entry on saponification](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saponification) documents the core reaction behind soap-making, one of this subfield's foundational techniques.
